summary: While InstantMed delivers clean copywriting and starts with a strong brand thesis ('Faster than your GP'), the conversion flow is crippled by a premature sign-in gate that blocks access to the clinical form. Serious mobile layout collisions between trust indicators and dynamic pills further degrade the premium feel of the experience.

brand_spine.observation: The brand core 'Telehealth without the small talk' is delivered confidently in the hero section, landing successfully with the 'Faster than your GP' tagline.

typography.observation: The font scaling on Plus Jakarta Sans looks solid, but the body paragraphs are set slightly too dense on mobile viewports, making the AHPRA disclaimer text feel cramped.

findings.issue: The body copy block underneath the subtitle has dense line-height and tight tracking, making the key AHPRA-registered text difficult to scan on mobile.
findings.recommendation: Increase the line-height of the body text to 1.5rem (24px) and add subtle letter-spacing to improve readability.

color_and_surface.observation: While the warm ivory background renders nicely on the home screen, the Sign In screen loses this warmth and transitions to a generic cool-gray palette.

findings.issue: The Sign In button uses a desaturated, washed-out purple/blue instead of the confident, high-contrast primary brand blue (#2563EB) seen on the home screen.
findings.recommendation: Unify the primary button styles by utilizing the high-contrast brand blue (#2563EB) for the Sign In action on the login screen.

motion.observation: Transitions between major viewports feel basic and unpolished, featuring a jarring blank screen lag instead of fluid transitions.

findings.issue: A prolonged, completely blank white screen appears for nearly two seconds when transitioning from the landing page to the login flow.
findings.recommendation: Implement a lightweight skeleton loader or an ivory-to-dawn gradient sweep to mask the network transition latency.

copy_voice.observation: The clinical voice feels reassuring and direct, but is missing critical price anchoring and refund terms in the hero copy.

findings.issue: The landing page copy fails to mention pricing or the standard 'Full refund if our doctor can't help' conversion guarantee.
findings.recommendation: Append small microcopy below the 'Get started' CTA: 'Form takes 3 mins. Full refund if we can't assist.'

hierarchy_and_layout.observation: The visual structure breaks down on mobile viewports, causing critical trust indicators to overlap and look cluttered.

findings.issue: The dynamic slide-up pills ('Medical certificate Approved' and 'eScript sent') overlap and obscure the secondary 'Doctor review' trust badges.
findings.recommendation: Apply a safe-area margin or adjust the absolute positioning of the dynamic overlay container to sit cleanly below the static trust text.

conversion_friction.observation: The product forces an immediate authentication gate right after the primary call-to-action, frustrating time-poor users expecting an instant form.

findings.issue: Clicking 'Get started' hits a hard wall requiring users to sign in or sign up before they can see or interact with the clinical intake form.
findings.recommendation: Defer sign-up entirely. Let users complete the 3-minute form first and prompt for account creation at the final submit step.

signature_devices.observation: The live wait-counter device ('Last reviewed 14 min ago') is present but feels static and isolated from the core CTA.

findings.issue: The platform lacks a highly specific live wait-counter near the CTA to prove real-time velocity.
findings.recommendation: Replace the static 'Last reviewed' label with a live metrics string: 'Average cert sent in 14 minutes today'.

top_three_actions.action: Defer the login wall past the clinical form
top_three_actions.why: At 00:11, forcing immediate login creates a high-friction gate. Postponing this to the end of the form will dramatically raise conversion rates.
At 00:02, the animated notification pills overlap and obscure secondary trust badges, making the landing page look broken on mobile devices.

top_three_actions.action: Add explicit price anchoring and guarantees
top_three_actions.why: At 00:01, there is zero mention of pricing or the refund policy near the main CTAs, which hurts trust on high-anxiety patient surfaces.
